title: IS-IS Adjacency and SPF Calculation Monitoring
dataSources: Cisco IOS / IOS-XR (`sourcetype=cisco:ios`) and Juniper Junos (`sourcetype=junos:syslog`) routing-protocol syslog. Key Cisco mnemonics: `%ISIS-5-ADJCHANGE` for adjacency transitions and `%ISIS-6-SPF_TRIG_NOT_BKOFF` (IOS-XR) for SPF-trigger pressure. Junos emits matching `RPD_ISIS_ADJCHANGE` events. Adjacency state and neighbour identity are extractable with rex from the message body.
app: Cisco Networks Add-on for Splunk (`TA-cisco_ios`, Splunkbase 1352), Splunk Add-on for Juniper (`Splunk_TA_juniper`, Splunkbase 2847)
spl:
index=network (sourcetype="cisco:ios" "%ISIS-5-ADJCHANGE") OR (sourcetype="junos:syslog" "RPD_ISIS_ADJCHANGE")
| rex "(?<isis_adj_state>UP|DOWN|INIT).*(?<neighbor>\S+)"
| stats count by host, neighbor, isis_adj_state
| where isis_adj_state="DOWN" OR count > 5
| sort - count
description: Tracks IS-IS adjacency state transitions and SPF-trigger frequency on routers running the IS-IS IGP. Surfaces routers and neighbour pairs whose adjacency dropped, and routers whose SPF-trigger rate exceeds normal noise — both early indicators of routing instability that converge into traffic outages within seconds if not addressed.
value: IS-IS is the dominant IGP in large-scale service-provider and modern data-centre networks where OSPF runs out of headroom around 1,000 routers. When an IS-IS adjacency drops, every router in the area runs SPF and recalculates its routing table. A few drops are normal hardware churn; sustained adjacency flapping is a hardware fault, an MTU mismatch on a new circuit, or an incipient hardware failure. SPF-trigger rate is the early warning — if SPF runs more than once a minute, fast-converging traffic-engineering paths are constantly being recalculated and forwarding-state churn starts losing packets. This UC is the leading indicator that catches these problems hours before the user-visible outage.
implementation: Enable IS-IS syslog on every IS-IS router at severity 5 or lower. Forward syslog through `TA-cisco_ios` / `Splunk_TA_juniper`. Alert on adjacency DOWN events and on SPF-trigger rates above the per-router 7-day baseline. Track the rate of `%ISIS-6-SPF_TRIG_NOT_BKOFF` on IOS-XR — elevated rates here are the leading indicator of network-wide instability.
visualization: Table (adjacency changes by router / neighbour), Timeline (SPF triggers per router, useful for spotting concurrent runs), Status grid (per-router adjacency state coloured red / amber / green).
knownFalsePositives:
**Scheduled circuit grooming.** ISP-side circuit grooming or BGP optimiser changes will flap underlying links and ripple through IS-IS. Suppress alerts during announced circuit-windows.

**LSP regeneration after metric change.** Any IS-IS metric change triggers a fleet-wide SPF burst as routers recompute. This is normal and the burst should subside within a minute. Threshold the SPF-trigger alert on rate > N per minute *sustained*, not on a single snapshot.

**Lab and test routers.** Hosts in lab address space frequently flap as engineers test config changes. Filter alerts to production loopback ranges only.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the routing protocol that the largest networks use to figure out how to send data between routers. When two routers stop talking to each other, every other router has to recalculate paths. We catch the talk-stops and the storm of recalculation early so engineers can fix the cause before traffic starts dropping.
